# ODE Landing Page Copy

## Hero Section

**Headline:** Engineer the Future with AI

**Subheadline:** ODE is the first AI-native hardware design platform. Model physics, simulate systems, and collaborate in real time -- all from your browser, with an AI co-pilot that actually understands engineering.

**Primary CTA:** Start Building for Free

**Secondary CTA:** Watch the Demo (2 min)

**Hero Visual Direction:** Split-screen showing a Modelica model on the left being generated by AI from a natural-language prompt, and a 3D simulation result rendering on the right. Animated, browser-based.

---

## Problem Section

### Engineering Tools Were Not Built for the AI Era

Hardware engineering is stuck. The tools you rely on were designed decades ago -- monolithic desktops apps, proprietary file formats, and zero intelligence built in.

**The cost of legacy tooling:**

- **Fragmented workflows.** You bounce between CAD, simulation, documentation, and systems engineering tools that do not talk to each other. Every handoff loses fidelity and wastes hours.

- **No AI, just marketing.** Legacy vendors bolt "AI features" onto 30-year-old architectures. Auto-meshing is not intelligence. You deserve tools that were designed from the ground up around AI.

- **Vendor lock-in.** Proprietary formats trap your IP. Collaboration means emailing files. Version control means appending "_v7_final_FINAL" to filenames.

- **Expensive and inaccessible.** Six-figure seat licenses shut out startups, academics, and independent engineers. Innovation should not require a procurement department.

> "We spend more time fighting our tools than designing our products."
> -- Every hardware engineer, eventually.

---

## Solution Section

### ODE: The AI-Native Design Platform

ODE brings six integrated engineering tools into a single browser-based platform, powered by AI that understands physics, systems, and your intent.

No installation. No file conversion. No waiting for IT.

Built on open standards (Modelica 4.0, FMI 2.0/3.0, SysML v1.6) so your work is never locked in.

**What "AI-native" actually means:**

ODE was not built and then had AI added. Every tool, every workflow, every data structure was designed so that AI can read, reason about, and generate engineering artifacts. The difference is fundamental -- like the difference between a horse-drawn carriage with a motor strapped on and a car designed around the engine.

---

## Feature Sections

### 1. Component -- Physics Modeling That Speaks Your Language

Build multi-domain physical models using Modelica 4.0, the international standard for equation-based modeling. Electrical, mechanical, thermal, hydraulic, and control systems in one unified environment.

**What you get:**

- Drag-and-drop component library with 1,400+ pre-built models from the Modelica Standard Library
- Equation-level editing for when you need full control
- AI-assisted model generation: describe what you need in plain English, get a working Modelica model
- Automatic model validation and dimensional analysis
- FMI 2.0/3.0 export for interoperability with any simulation tool

**Why it matters:** Stop translating between physics domains. Model your system the way it actually works -- as interconnected physical phenomena, not isolated discipline silos.

---

### 2. Paper -- Engineering Documentation, Alive

Collaborative documents that stay connected to your models, simulations, and design decisions. Not a generic doc tool -- purpose-built for engineering teams.

**What you get:**

- Real-time collaborative editing with engineering-aware formatting
- Live embeds: drag in a model, simulation result, or 3D view and it stays synced
- Version history with diff visualization for technical content
- Review and approval workflows built in
- AI-powered writing assistance that understands engineering terminology and context

**Why it matters:** Documentation is where institutional knowledge lives or dies. Paper makes it effortless to capture decisions, share context, and keep records that actually reflect the current state of your design.

---

### 3. System -- Model-Based Systems Engineering, Simplified

Define system architectures, requirements, and interfaces using SysML v1.6 -- without needing a PhD in MBSE methodology.

**What you get:**

- Visual system architecture diagrams with drag-and-drop block definition
- Requirements management with traceability to models and tests
- Interface definition and compatibility checking across subsystems
- AI-assisted architecture generation from high-level descriptions
- Export to standard SysML formats for enterprise integration

**Why it matters:** Systems engineering should clarify complexity, not add to it. System makes MBSE accessible to every engineer on the team, not just the two people who went to the training.

---

### 4. Vision -- See Your System Before You Build It

High-fidelity 3D simulation and visualization running in your browser. No GPU workstation required.

**What you get:**

- Real-time 3D rendering of system behavior driven by your Modelica models
- Multi-physics visualization: see thermal gradients, stress distributions, and flow patterns overlaid on geometry
- Parametric studies with instant visual feedback
- Cloud-rendered simulations that scale with your problem, not your hardware budget
- Shareable simulation links for stakeholder reviews -- no software install needed

**Why it matters:** Understanding comes from seeing. Vision turns abstract equations into intuitive visual experiences that accelerate design decisions and make reviews productive.

---

### 5. Geometry -- CAD That Connects to Everything

Parametric 3D geometry creation and editing, integrated with your physics models and system architecture.

**What you get:**

- Browser-based parametric CAD with a modern, responsive interface
- Bidirectional links between geometry parameters and Modelica model variables
- Import/export for STEP, IGES, STL, and other standard formats
- AI-assisted geometry generation from sketches or descriptions
- Version-controlled geometry with branching and merging

**Why it matters:** Geometry is not an island. When your CAD parameters drive your simulation and your simulation informs your geometry, you close the loop that legacy tools leave open.

---

### 6. Interaction -- Share, Present, Explore in Real Time

Interactive visualization and presentation tool for design reviews, stakeholder communication, and collaborative exploration.

**What you get:**

- Build interactive dashboards from simulation results, 3D views, and design parameters
- Real-time multi-user sessions: everyone sees the same view, anyone can interact
- Adjustable parameters with instant feedback -- answer "what if?" questions live in meetings
- Export to standalone interactive reports
- Embed in presentations, wikis, or customer portals

**Why it matters:** Great engineering dies in bad presentations. Interaction lets you show, not tell -- and lets stakeholders explore your design space instead of passively watching slides.

---

## AI Capabilities Section

### Your AI Engineering Co-Pilot

ODE integrates Claude AI with a purpose-built Modelica MCP Server to deliver AI capabilities that go far beyond chatbot-style assistance. This is AI that understands physics.

**Generate models from requirements:**
Describe your system in natural language. "I need a thermal model of a battery pack with 12 cells, liquid cooling, and ambient heat loss." ODE generates a complete, validated Modelica model with correct physics, proper units, and sensible default parameters.

**Run and interpret simulations:**
Ask questions about your design. "What happens to cell temperature if coolant flow drops by 50%?" The AI configures the simulation, runs it, and explains the results in engineering terms -- with plots, not just numbers.

**Analyze and optimize:**
"Find the minimum coolant flow rate that keeps all cells below 45C at maximum discharge." The AI sets up parametric sweeps, identifies the design space boundaries, and recommends operating points.

**Debug and validate:**
Stuck on a model that will not converge? The AI reads your equations, identifies common issues (index reduction problems, inconsistent initial conditions, singular systems), and suggests specific fixes.

**Learn and explain:**
New to Modelica? The AI explains what every component does, why specific modeling choices were made, and how the physics connects to real-world behavior. Like having a senior engineer available 24/7.

> Explore the AI capabilities yourself: [ODE AI Demo on GitHub](https://github.com/dennisyangji/ODE_AI_Demo)

## FAQ Section

### Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is ODE really free to use?**
A: Yes. The Free tier gives you full access to all six tools with generous AI usage limits. No credit card required, no time limit. We believe the best way to prove our value is to let you experience it.

**Q: What is Modelica, and why should I care?**
A: Modelica is an open, equation-based modeling language standardized internationally (Modelica Association). It lets you model multi-domain physical systems (electrical, mechanical, thermal, hydraulic, control) in a single unified framework. Unlike proprietary alternatives, your Modelica models are portable and future-proof.

**Q: How does ODE's AI compare to ChatGPT or Copilot for engineering?**
A: General-purpose AI tools can discuss engineering concepts but cannot create, validate, or simulate actual engineering models. ODE's AI integrates Claude with a purpose-built Modelica MCP Server that understands physics equations, dimensional analysis, and simulation semantics. It generates models that compile and run, not just code that looks plausible.

**Q: Can I import my existing CAD files and models?**
A: Yes. ODE supports import of STEP, IGES, and STL geometry files, as well as Modelica libraries and FMI 2.0/3.0 FMUs. We are actively expanding format support based on user feedback.

**Q: Is my data secure?**
A: ODE uses enterprise-grade security: data encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), SOC 2 Type II compliance (in progress), GDPR-compliant data handling, and optional on-premise deployment for Enterprise customers. Your engineering IP is your most valuable asset -- we treat it that way.

**Q: Does ODE work offline?**
A: ODE is browser-based and requires an internet connection for full functionality. Enterprise customers can deploy ODE on-premise or in a private cloud for air-gapped environments.

**Q: How does ODE handle version control?**
A: Every artifact in ODE (models, geometry, documents, system architectures) is version-controlled with full history, branching, and merging. Think Git, but purpose-built for engineering data.

**Q: Can I use ODE with my existing simulation tools?**
A: Yes. ODE supports FMI 2.0 and 3.0 (Functional Mock-up Interface), the international standard for simulation model exchange. Export FMUs from ODE and import them into MATLAB/Simulink, Dymola, or any FMI-compliant tool -- and vice versa.

**Q: What kind of support do you offer?**
A: Free users get community support (forum and Discord). Starter and Pro users get email support with 24-hour response times. Team users get dedicated support with 4-hour response times. Enterprise users get a named customer success manager and SLA-backed support.

**Q: Is there an API?**
A: Yes. Pro tier and above include API access for programmatic model creation, simulation execution, and result retrieval. Full API documentation is available at docs.orthogonal.dev.
